label: Statistics & experimental design

node.label: Statistics & experimental design
node.definition: Estimation, hypothesis testing, sampling, and experimental design connect finite observations to general claims.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Statistics & experimental design is modeled as depending on Probability theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Causal inference, Databases & information retrieval, Datasets, sampling & splits.
examples: atlas downstream link to Causal inference
atlas downstream link to Databases & information retrieval
atlas downstream link to Datasets, sampling & splits
limitations: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory.

dependencies.rationale: Statistics & experimental design rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.
